techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained. Brute forcing passwords can take place via interaction with a service that will check the validity of those credentials.
techniques.detection: Monitor authentication logs for repeated failed login attempts from the same source IP in a short time window.
mitigations: Block repeated failed login attempts at the firewall
Monitor and alert on abnormal authentication activity

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery / Port Scan
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices by scanning ports to identify open services and potential vulnerabilities.
techniques.detection: Monitor network traffic for rapid sequential connection attempts across multiple ports from a single source IP.
mitigations: Use firewalls to block unsolicited connection attempts
Monitor and alert on port scanning behavior
Segment networks to limit scanning scope

techniques.name: Valid Accounts / Suspicious Login
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts to gain initial access. Compromised credentials may be used to bypass access controls and gain access to systems.
techniques.detection: Monitor for successful logins following multiple failed attempts from the same source IP, logins from unusual geolocations, or logins during off-hours targeting admin accounts.
mitigations: Review and audit account permissions regularly
techniques.tactic: Defense Evasion, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Initial Access
